fetch_attempts.error_summary: ConnectionResetError(10054, 'An existing connection was forcibly closed by the remote host'); transient mid-stream failure
fetch_attempts.side_warning_observed_before_failure: BentoWarning: streaming request contained reduced-quality days (2020-02-27 degraded, 2020-02-28 degraded, 2020-06-30 degraded, and possibly more). Warning is informational about source quality, not the cause of the failure.

fetch_attempts.error_summary: Read timed out at 100 seconds (default databento client read_timeout)

fetch_outcome_reason: Two consecutive transient network failures before any CSV row was written

halt_root_cause.description: Two consecutive controller-side streaming fetch attempts against Databento Historical API hist.databento.com both failed with transient network-level errors before any CSV row was materialized to disk. The first attempt aborted mid-stream with a ConnectionResetError; the second timed out at the default 100s read timeout. Both failures were observed only on the MNQ.c.0 symbol; the MGC.c.0 fetch was never attempted because the script fails closed on the first symbol failure.
halt_root_cause.operator_authorization_text: If Databento fetch fails, HALT and report.

possible_next_steps_documented.description: Operator may re-authorize the same fetch turn at a later time when network conditions are more favorable.

Fetch in smaller date windows (e.g., year-by-year or quarter-by-quarter) and concatenate. Smaller requests are less likely to hit read timeouts. Would require a separately authorized turn with a revised tooling design that may or may not match the operator's previous fetch path.

The parallel-session fetch tool at tools/operator_side/s10_d1_mnq_mgc_step02b_fetch_databento.py (committed at a8df18b110023f08e147137df565522426d074db) is designed to be operator-side run with the API key in the operator's local environment. Operator can run that tool in their own shell, then place the resulting CSVs in the approved output directory, and this controller-session can later seal a Step 02b ingest report by reference without making any Databento call.

S10-D2 reuses the existing s8-D1 cache (no new Databento fetch required for D2's 4-symbol full-futures universe at $500K start). Operator may pivot to D2 if D1 fetch infrastructure proves unreliable on this client.
Stop here, do not retry, do not pivot. Wait for operator direction. This is the default if no other action is authorized.

seal_method: sha256 over json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":"), ensure_ascii=False) EXCLUDING report_seal_sha256 + seal_method
